deep_dive.title: Fine-grained Computation-Communication Overlap via Tile-level Signaling and Scheduling for Mixture-of-Experts
deep_dive.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures increase model capacity without proportionally increasing computation cost and have become a key building block for scaling large language models (LLMs) to trillion-parameter regimes. Efficient deployment of these MoE models relies on distributed execution across multiple GPUs, where each MoE layer involves two all-to-all communications: dispatching tokens to expert ranks and returning the expert outputs to their source ranks. Conventional MoE implementations launch this return all-to-all after expert compute completes, exposing communication latency on the critical path and reducing GPU utilization. We present a fine-grained approach that overlaps expert compute with the second all-to-all via tile-level signaling and scheduling. Our producer-consumer co-design combines: (1) a persistent per-rank computation kernel (producer) that covers all local experts on the rank to eliminate repeated kernel launch overhead and prioritizes remote-critical tiles, and (2) a persistent communication kernel (consumer) on a small dedicated partition of streaming multiprocessors (SMs) that issues segment-granular transfers as tiles become ready. Our co-design avoids intrusive changes to the underlying computation operators or communication primitives, making it practical for improving distributed MoE execution efficiency on multi-GPU systems. On a 4-A100 GPU platform, evaluated on three MoE models against four state-of-the-art MoE systems, our approach achieves up to 2.64x end-to-end speedup and 2.74x MoE-layer speedup. Compared with a conventional non-overlap baseline, our approach consistently improves both operator- and MoE-layer-level performance across varying GEMM shapes, router modes, and a broad range of producer/consumer SM partitions, while preserving correctness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: optimizes MoE communication with tile-level scheduling
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents a fine-grained approach that overlaps expert computation with the second all-to-all communication in MoE layers via tile-level signaling and scheduling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: MoE模型部署在多GPU上时，全收集通信成为性能瓶颈。本文提出细粒度方法，通过tile级信令和调度将专家计算与第二次全收集通信重叠，减少关键路径延迟。实验表明可显著提升GPU利用率和吞吐量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: MoE模型部署在多GPU上时，全收集通信成为性能瓶颈。本文提出细粒度方法，通过tile级信令和调度将专家计算与第二次全收集通信重叠，减少关键路径延迟。实验表明可显著提升GPU利用率和吞吐量。

deep_dive.title: MoX: Efficient MoE Routing on Direct-Connect Topologies
deep_dive.abstract: Optically switched networks suit the regular communication of dense ML models, but MoE introduces sparse, runtime-dependent traffic. We show that efficient offline-optimized routing enables efficient MoE training and inference on direct-connect topologies without the need for MoE traffic matrix or dynamic topology reconfiguration. MoX constructs token-aware multicast trees to reduce bandwidth tax, then uses static, precomputed link weights to balance traffic by solving a restricted multicast tree-packing problem. Using recorded traffic from large MoE models, token-level traces, and ASTRA-sim, we find that MoX accelerates the full MoE block -- dispatch, expert computation, and combine -- by up to 1.8x over min-hop routing. Moreover, it attains nearly ideal packet-switched network performance in random expander topologies. On a 1,024-TPU model of Google's Boardfly topology, MoX reduces the dispatch bottleneck link load by up to 47%. These results show that high-performance MoE on static direct-connect fabrics can be achieved via optimized load-oblivious routing without demand-driven reconfiguration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MoX optimizes MoE routing on direct-connect topologies using token-aware multicast trees and static link weights, achieving up to 1.8x speedup for the full MoE block.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 混合专家模型（MoE）在直连拓扑上因稀疏动态流量导致通信效率低下。MoX通过构建token感知多播树和离线优化静态链路权重，无需动态重配置即可高效路由。在大型MoE模型流量轨迹上的实验显示，MoX将完整MoE块（分发、专家计算、合并）加速达1.8倍，并接近理想分组交换性能。该工作为MoE在大规模分布式系统中的高效训练推理提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 混合专家模型（MoE）在直连拓扑上因稀疏动态流量导致通信效率低下。MoX通过构建token感知多播树和离线优化静态链路权重，无需动态重配置即可高效路由。在大型MoE模型流量轨迹上的实验显示，MoX将完整MoE块（分发、专家计算、合并）加速达1.8倍，并接近理想分组交换性能。该工作为MoE在大规模分布式系统中的高效训练推理提供了实用方案。

deep_dive.title: MoAKE: Toward Unified All-in-One Action Quality Assessment via Mixture of Action Knowledge Experts
deep_dive.abstract: Action Quality Assessment (AQA) aims to objectively evaluate performance quality from action videos. Most existing methods follow a ``one-by-one'' paradigm, training a separate model for each action type. This setting limits real-world deployment, as it requires prior action-type knowledge to select the corresponding model and suffers from poor generalization across diverse actions. To address these limitations, we study the challenging task of all-in-one AQA, which aims to assess heterogeneous actions within a single unified model. We propose a novel Mixture of Action Knowledge Experts (MoAKE) framework, designed to mitigate negative knowledge transfer caused by large semantic discrepancies among actions. MoAKE learns complementary experts that capture diverse action patterns within a shared semantic space and dynamically aggregates their knowledge to adapt the assessment to the input action. Each expert is tailored with segment-aware prototypes to handle varying temporal lengths, together with an Adaptive Intra- and Inter-Segment Relationship Modeling (AIISRM) module to model multi-granularity temporal dynamics. Furthermore, we establish comprehensive benchmarks for all-in-one as well as zero/few-shot AQA. Extensive experiments on three long-term datasets demonstrate that MoAKE significantly outperforms existing methods in the all-in-one setting, while also achieving consistent generalization on three short-term datasets under zero/few-shot evaluation. Code is available at https://github.com/XuHuangbiao/MoAKE.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: mixture of action knowledge experts for heterogeneous actions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对不同动作的混合动作知识专家
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对不同动作的混合动作知识专家
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对不同动作的混合动作知识专家
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MoAKE introduces a mixture of action knowledge experts for unified action quality assessment, mitigating negative knowledge transfer across heterogeneous actions and achieving SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有动作质量评估方法为每种动作单独训练模型，部署能力受限。本文提出MoAKE框架，采用混合动作知识专家统一评估多种动作，有效缓解负知识迁移。实验表明，MoAKE在多个数据集上达到最优性能。该工作展示了混合专家在动作评估中的巨大潜力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有动作质量评估方法为每种动作单独训练模型，部署能力受限。本文提出MoAKE框架，采用混合动作知识专家统一评估多种动作，有效缓解负知识迁移。实验表明，MoAKE在多个数据集上达到最优性能。该工作展示了混合专家在动作评估中的巨大潜力。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: expert specialization in mixture of experts

deep_dive.title: PerceptDrive: Perception Prior World-Action Modeling with Adaptive Expert Routing for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
deep_dive.abstract: Frozen perception foundation models encode rich geometric, semantic, and dynamic knowledge. Yet narrow conditioning interfaces may attenuate task-relevant cues, while static fusion cannot adjust expert contributions to each scene. We cast this challenge as the prior-to-plan transfer problem and introduce PerceptDrive, a perception prior world-action modeling framework with adaptive expert routing. PerceptDrive feeds teacher-distilled priors from a frozen, driving-adapted provider and dense observation latents from a frozen self-supervised video encoder into a trainable expert-routed world-action model. Expert-specific query branches process these signals, while a prior-retention objective anchors each branch to its prior. A router predicts soft gates from a shared scene representation and combines the expert conditions before trajectory generation. During training, privileged rule-based sub-metric estimates for branch-specific trajectory drafts provide soft-gate distillation targets. The predicted action-free future latent conditions a flow-matching actor. At inference, privileged components are absent; with one front-facing camera, PerceptDrive generates one trajectory per planning step without test-time scoring, reranking, or search. Experiments show that PerceptDrive achieves state-of-the-art performance with 90.4 PDMS on NAVSIM v1 and 90.2 EPDMS on NAVSIM v2, outperforming existing methods. Ablations confirm complementary gains from prior retention and scene-conditioned routing, alongside differential reliance on the three priors. These results demonstrate that preserving and adaptively routing perception priors improves direct planning without test-time candidate selection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adaptive expert routing for autonomous driving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自动驾驶中的自适应专家路由
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自动驾驶中的自适应专家路由
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自动驾驶中的自适应专家路由
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PerceptDrive uses adaptive expert routing to fuse perception priors and observation latents, improving end-to-end driving planning by dynamically adjusting expert contributions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有端到端自动驾驶方法在融合感知先验时无法适应场景变化。PerceptDrive提出自适应专家路由框架，利用冻结的感知编码器提取先验和观测信息，通过可训练的专家路由模型动态调整各专家贡献。实验表明该方法显著提升了规划性能，验证了自适应专家路由在复杂驾驶场景中的有效性。该工作为感知先验到规划决策的迁移提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有端到端自动驾驶方法在融合感知先验时无法适应场景变化。PerceptDrive提出自适应专家路由框架，利用冻结的感知编码器提取先验和观测信息，通过可训练的专家路由模型动态调整各专家贡献。实验表明该方法显著提升了规划性能，验证了自适应专家路由在复杂驾驶场景中的有效性。该工作为感知先验到规划决策的迁移提供了新思路。
